briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings after the collapse of Synapse, exposing serious risks in fintech middleware and banking-as-a-service models.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep dive into how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of Credit Kudos signals growing Big Tech interest in leveraging open banking data for consumer financial services.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit card products in increasingly unconventional directions, blurring the lines between lending, rewards, and embedded finance.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[BDC's new $500-million loan program will help smaller businesses adopt AI](https://betakit.com/bdcs-new-500-million-loan-program-will-help-smaller-businesses-adopt-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada is launching a $500-million loan program to support small and medium-sized businesses in adopting artificial intelligence tools.

- **[Canada's AI regulation will be "airtight" on bias, racism, and hate, Solomon says](https://betakit.com/canadas-ai-regulation-will-be-airtight-on-bias-racism-and-hate-solomon-says/)** — *BetaKit* — A Canadian government official signals that forthcoming AI regulation will take a firm stance against algorithmic bias and discriminatory outputs.

- **[Former Startup TNT leader launches coalition for Canada's early-stage investors](https://betakit.com/former-startup-tnt-leader-launches-coalition-for-canadas-early-stage-investors/)** — *BetaKit* — A new coalition is forming to advocate for and strengthen Canada's early-stage venture and angel investment ecosystem.

- **[Canada's new US economic advisory committee draws backlash from tech leaders](https://betakit.com/canadas-new-us-economic-advisory-committee-draws-backlash-from-tech-leaders/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian tech leaders are pushing back on a newly formed advisory committee focused on US-Canada economic relations, raising concerns about sovereignty and industry representation.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* *(Global, relevant to Canada)* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, a foundational moment still shaping Canadian open banking policy today.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canadian AI firm Cohere to merge with Germany's Aleph Alpha](https://financialpost.com/technology/cohere-to-merge-german-ai-aleph-alpha)** — *Financial Post* — Toronto-based AI firm Cohere is merging with German AI startup Aleph Alpha in a deal backed by $600 million in investment, creating a major transatlantic AI enterprise with implications for Canadian tech leadership and fintech AI infrastructure.

- **[BDC's new $500-million loan program will help smaller businesses adopt AI](https://betakit.com/bdcs-new-500-million-loan-program-will-help-smaller-businesses-adopt-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — BDC's new lending program is directly relevant to Atlantic Canadian fintech startups and SMEs looking to integrate AI into their financial products and operations.

- **[Former Startup TNT leader launches coalition for Canada's early-stage investors](https://betakit.com/former-startup-tnt-leader-launches-coalition-for-canadas-early-stage-investors/)** — *BetaKit* — A new national coalition for early-stage investors could benefit Atlantic Canada's fintech ecosystem, where access to seed and angel capital remains a persistent challenge for emerging startups.
